codeReview.summary: Clean, correct implementation of click-to-position. Three files changed: compositor MSG_POINTER_BUTTON handler (68 lines), editor MSG_SET_CURSOR handler (13 lines), and 4 new unit tests (78 lines). The compositor performs z-order hit testing (title bar excluded, image mode excluded), converts screen coordinates to content-relative text coordinates via saturating_sub (handles margin/shadow clicks gracefully), applies scroll offset, and delegates to TextLayout::xy_to_byte for pixel-to-byte conversion. Cursor position is updated directly (compositor is sole writer), selection is cleared, document header is written, and MSG_SET_CURSOR (type 35) is sent to the editor to keep its local cursor in sync. The editor handler is correctly placed before the MSG_KEY_EVENT filter so it isn't silently dropped. All coordinate math uses u32 with saturating arithmetic, avoiding overflow. The text_changed flag is correctly set to trigger both scroll offset recalculation and content surface re-render (cursor needs redrawing at new position). Tests cover round-trip consistency, past-end clamping, below-text clamping, and scroll offset adjustment — all at the appropriate TextLayout abstraction level.

issues.description: The Editor ↔ Compositor IPC Protocol table in architecture.md does not include the new MSG_SET_CURSOR (type 35, compositor → editor) message. The table should be updated to document this message type so future workers can discover it without reading compositor source.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture.md Editor ↔ Compositor IPC Protocol table is missing MSG_SET_CURSOR (type 35, compositor → editor, payload: CursorMove { position: u32 }). This message was added in this feature but the documentation wasn't updated. Future workers implementing pointer features or editor enhancements will need to know about this message.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md — IPC table lists types 1, 30-34 but not 35. system/services/compositor/main.rs:52 defines MSG_SET_CURSOR = 35.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker suggested that the os-worker skill should note that xy_to_byte already exists in the drawing library. This is a valid observation — a future worker implementing click-related features would benefit from knowing this function exists without having to discover it via grep. The worker followed procedure correctly and surfaced this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The os-worker skill could note that xy_to_byte already exists in the drawing library — a previous worker added it. This would save time for future click-related features.'

summary: Pass. The click-to-position implementation is correct and complete. All six expected behaviors from the feature spec are covered: (1) click in content area positions cursor via coordinate conversion + xy_to_byte, (2) clicks past end of text clamp to end of nearest line (delegated to xy_to_byte which returns text.len()), (3) title bar clicks are ignored via explicit check, (4) typing after click inserts at new position because both compositor and editor update cursor, (5) keyboard and mouse coexist without conflict since MSG_SET_CURSOR is handled separately from MSG_KEY_EVENT, (6) all 900 tests pass. One non-blocking documentation gap: architecture.md IPC table needs MSG_SET_CURSOR added.
